name: NBR — Neutralization-Based Reclamation
summary: Singh, Brown, Mashtizadeh (PPoPP 2021) use an EBR-like fast path; when the thread-local retire buffer overflows, the reclaimer sends a POSIX signal to all threads, which respond by re-validating their position (handshake) — neutralising any thread holding stale references. Bounds memory like HP but reclamation speed like EBR (38% faster than DEBRA on BST, 243% faster than HP on lazy linked-list). No record-layout changes, no recovery code.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Singh, Brown, Mashtizadeh's **Neutralization-Based Reclamation** (PPoPP 2021) uses an EBR-like fast path; when the thread-local retire buffer overflows, the reclaimer sends a POSIX signal to all threads, which respond by re-validating their position (a handshake) — "neutralising" any thread holding stale references.

Result: bounds memory like HP but reclamation speed like EBR. Reported wins: 38% faster than DEBRA on a binary search tree benchmark; 243% faster than HP on a lazy linked-list. **No record-layout changes, no recovery code** — the data structure does not need to be modified to support NBR, unlike VBR which requires version stamps in every node.

Works for many lock-based as well as lock-free structures using only atomic R/W/CAS, broadening its applicability beyond classical lock-free SMR territory.
